commands.review_summary:
This PR changes `talk.config` to use matching runtime-resolved provider config before redaction, adds Talk SecretRef regression coverage, changes the thread-memory QA scenario, and adds a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +144, Docs -107, Other +100. Total +157 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence runtime repro was run in this read-only review. Source inspection shows the remaining provider-divergence edge clearly enough to keep the PR open, but the branch still needs real after-fix behavior proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human handling is needed because the remaining useful fix is narrow, but the branch is conflicted, proof is missing, and maintainers must choose refresh versus replacement.

target.title: fix(doctor): suppress false-positive group allowlist warning for parent-only channel records (#92684)
target.body:
## Summary

Fix false-positive doctor warning: when a channel has per-account configs with
populated `groupAllowFrom` lists, the empty top-level `groupAllowFrom` no longer
triggers a misleading "all group messages will be silently dropped" warning.

## Root Cause

`scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` called `checkAccount` on the top-level
channel record as if it were a standalone account — even when active per-account
sub-records existed. When the top-level `groupAllowFrom` was empty (serving only
as a fallback), the doctor treated it as an unconfigured account and emitted the
group-drop warning. In reality, every account supplied its own populated
`groupAllowFrom`, so the top-level empty list was never read by the runtime.

Fix: when the channel has active (non-disabled) per-account configs, pass
`suppressGroupWarning=true` to the top-level `checkAccount` call. This skips
only the default empty-group-allowlist warning for parent-only records; DM
allowlist warnings and all account-level warnings remain unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior
Doctor no longer emits a false-positive group-allowlist warning for an empty
top-level `groupAllowFrom` when every per-account record carries its own
populated allowlist. Non-regression: DM allowlist warnings still fire on parent
records, and group warnings still fire when no per-account configs exist.

### environment
- OS: Windows 10 Enterprise LTSC 2019
- Runtime: Node.js 22.x, pnpm
- Setup: openclaw workspace at fix commit (32ece257), vitest v4.1.7

### steps
1. Configure a channel with `groupPolicy: "allowlist"`, empty top-level
   `groupAllowFrom`, and accounts each with populated `groupAllowFrom`.
2. Run `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` with the config via vitest.
3. Observe: 0 warnings emitted (previously 1 false-positive).
4. Verify non-regression: channel without accounts still warns; DM allowlist
   warning still fires on parent records.

### observedResult

**Before fix (main branch) — false-positive warning present:**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/repro-92684.test.ts

stdout | repro-92684.test.ts > BEFORE-FIX
Warnings emitted: 1
  - channels.telegram.groupPolicy is "allowlist" but groupAllowFrom
    (and allowFrom) is empty — all group messages will be silently
    dropped. Add sender IDs to channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom or
    channels.telegram.allowFrom, or set groupPolicy to "open".

 FAIL  BEFORE-FIX: false-positive group warning
AssertionError: expected [ Array(1) ] to deeply equal []
```

**After fix (fix branch) — no false-positive, all 39 tests pass:**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/commands/doctor/shared/repro-92684.test.ts \
    src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts \
    src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  19 passed (19)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
```

**Reproduction test suite (3 new tests covering the fix):**

| Test | Description | Result |
|------|-------------|--------|
| BEFORE-FIX | Top-level empty `groupAllowFrom` + populated per-account allowlists → 0 warnings | ✅ PASS |
| No-accounts fallback | Top-level empty `groupAllowFrom` + no accounts → still warns | ✅ PASS |
| DM warning preserved | Top-level `dmPolicy: allowlist` + empty `allowFrom` + accounts → DM warning still fires | ✅ PASS |

**Production change (`src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.ts`):**
```diff
+    const accounts = asObjectRecord(channelConfig.accounts);
+    const hasActiveAccounts =
+      accounts &&
+      Object.values(accounts).some((a) => !isDisabledRecord(a));
+
+    // When the channel has active per-account configs, the top-level record
+    // serves only as a parent fallback. Suppress the default
+    // empty-group-allowlist warning.
     checkAccount(
       channelConfig,
       `channels.${channelName}`,
       channelName,
+      undefined,
+      hasActiveAccounts,
     );
-
-    const accounts = asObjectRecord(channelConfig.accounts);
```

And in `checkAccount`:
```diff
+    const resolvedSkipGroup =
+      suppressGroupWarning
+        ? () => true
+        : params.shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning;
```

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts` — 3 passed
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts` — 13 passed
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/repro-92684.test.ts` — 3 passed (new, reproduces #92684)
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts` — 20 passed
- [x] Change is minimal (single file, 2 logical changes)

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
target.author: sheyanmin
